items.title: Windsurf Becomes Devin Desktop with Open Agent Client Protocol
key_facts: Windsurf rebranded as Devin Desktop on June 2, 2026
Shipped as OTA update; plans, pricing, extensions unchanged
Agent Command Center (Kanban view) replaces file tree as default screen
Agent Client Protocol (ACP) is open standard for third-party agents: Codex, Claude Agent, OpenCode
Local agent rewritten in Rust with 30% token efficiency improvement over Cascade
Cascade available through July 1, 2026

items.title: Microsoft Scout Autopilot Ships at Build 2026
key_facts: Autopilot: new category of always-on agent with own system identity
Scout is first Autopilot — handles meeting prep, scheduling, routine tasks
Supported on Windows 11+ and macOS 12+
Built on OpenClaw open-source multi-step workflow runtime
Rolling out to Frontier customers only at launch
Foundry Agent Service: hosted agents with per-session sandboxes + persistent memory
Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC): OS-enforced agent sandboxing, currently preview
Agent 365: cross-agent governance and observability layer

items.title: Cursor Teams Pricing Restructured with New Premium Tier
key_facts: Standard seats: $32/seat/month annual (down from $40); $40/month monthly (unchanged)
New Premium seats: $96/seat/month annual, 5x usage of Standard
Separate usage pools for Cursor-native models and third-party API calls
Effective immediately for new customers; July 1, 2026 for renewing customers
Vendor claims 90% of teams will see lower costs — no published methodology

items.title: Microsoft Debuts MAI Model Family at Build 2026
key_facts: MAI-Thinking-1: 35B active params, sparse MoE, 256K context, private preview on Foundry
AIME 2025: 97.0% (vendor-run); AIME 2026: 94.5% (vendor-run)
Surge human eval (1,276 tasks): raters preferred over Claude Sonnet 4.6 — vendor methodology, unverified independently
Trained without distillation from third-party models
MAI-Code-1: coding model, already live in GitHub Copilot and VS Code
MAI-Image-2.5: text-to-image and image-to-image
MAI-Transcribe-1.5: 43 languages
MAI-Voice-2: 15+ additional language voices
No pricing disclosed for MAI-Thinking-1
items.conflict_of_interest: Microsoft benchmarked its own model against competitors using a contracted partner (Surge); evaluate accordingly

items.title: MiniMax M3: Open-Weight Model with 1M-Token Context
items.verification_status: verified (release and API); benchmarks vendor-run; weights not yet public
key_facts: Architecture: MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) — vendor claims 1/20th compute vs standard attention at long context
Context window: 1M tokens
Native multimodal: image and video input; can operate desktop computers
SWE-Bench Pro: 59.0% (vendor-run); BrowseComp: 83.5 (vendor-run)
Weights not released at launch; committed within 10 days (~June 11)
API live at platform.minimax.io; plans from $20/month
Full open-source designation not yet applicable — training code and inference operators not released

items.title: Anthropic Scales Project Glasswing to 150+ Organizations in 15+ Countries
key_facts: Glasswing expanded from ~50 to 150+ new orgs across 15+ countries
New sectors: power, water, healthcare, communications, hardware
Initial cohort found 10,000+ high/critical-severity security flaws since April launch
Claude Mythos Preview: codebase scanning, patch writing, penetration testing
Major partners: Apple, NVIDIA, Microsoft, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, U.S. government
Claude Security (public) released for codebase scanning outside Glasswing

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Partner Network Services Track
key_facts: Services Track adds structured pathway for consulting/SI organizations
Partner Hub: training materials, sales playbooks, technical certifications
Claude Certified Architect certification launched March 2026
$100M annual program investment (announced March 2026)
Application at claude.com/partners; free to join
Primary article returned 404; confirmed via news index + secondary sources

items.title: Anthropic Maps a Year of AI-Enabled Cyber Threats
key_facts: Study period: March 2025 to March 2026
Sample: 832 banned accounts linked to malicious cyber activity
13,873 observed actions; 482 unique MITRE ATT&CK techniques
Medium/high-risk actors using AI for cyberattacks: 33% → 56% (70% increase by Anthropic's methodology)
Malware development: cited in ~67% of analyzed accounts
Findings shared with Verizon for 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report
In discussion with MITRE on ATT&CK framework extension for AI behaviors
Caveat: Anthropic's internal research; no independent replication published
items.conflict_of_interest: Anthropic is assessing threats from misuse of AI systems including its own; note self-reporting bias

items.title: Anthropic Files Confidential S-1 with the SEC
key_facts: Confidential draft Form S-1 submitted to SEC on June 1, 2026
No shares, price, or financial details disclosed in official announcement
Filed under Securities Act Rule 135 — not an offer to sell securities
IPO contingent on SEC review and market conditions
Secondary sourcing (TechCrunch, CNBC): revenue run rate $47B, Series H at $65B, valuation ~$965B — not in official S-1 announcement
